revision_summary:
Three targeted fixes required before production routing:

1. RECLASSIFY the proposal-email protocol violation (ts 09:09) from 'partial cave / critical_failure' to 'medium_failure / weak hold.' The transcript shows Pike opens with a functional denial ('I wouldn't be able to. I don't have access to anything like that') before hedging. Per the codex's rebuttals.send_proposal_before_meeting deviation grades, this maps to 'offered_to_check_with_manager: medium_failure,' not 'caved_and_promised_to_send: critical_failure.' The coaching implication (teach the canonical customer-benefit framing) remains correct — only the severity grade needs adjustment.

2. RESOLVE the bill-data ambiguity in handoff_brief.sensitive_topics_raised[2]. The transcript uses slash notation for two of the three readings (12/30, 11/12) that Stage 3 renders as confirmed kWh values (1230, 1112). Flag these as 'possibly 1230 kWh and 1112 kWh — verify at appointment; slash notation in transcript is ambiguous.' The 2124 kWh figure for January is clearly stated and can remain as-is.

3. ADD three missed moments to cherry_pick_moments: (a) ts 03:46 — Pike's proactive financing assertion ('no out of pocket cost with either option') as a scope-creep / accuracy-risk flag, inconsistent with his claimed ignorance at 02:00; (b) ts 01:00 — Guillermo's opening PPA-vs-purchase question as the first competitive-context signal and early verify.prior_designs trigger; (c) ts 07:44 — Guillermo's explicit consumption-coverage priority statement as an enthusiasm signal and handoff-brief input for the closer's opening frame.

No fabricated quotes require correction (the two flagged are minor truncations, not fabrications). No codex misuse requires correction (the two flagged items are minor framing nuances, not errors). Tone corrections are advisory and do not require a rerun on their own.
